observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Monitor the cashbooks of Accountable Officers and check regularly the correctness and appropriateness of entries therein; and
Expedite the reconciliation of cashbook-cash in bank and update regularly the transactions therein.
Ensure that the rules on the recording and safekeeping of cashbooks are strictly followed by all Accountable Officers.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the figures reflected in the cashbook and the books of accounts were accurate and reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated Municipal Treasurer to:

Direct all Revenue Collectors to prepare their respective monthly the RAAFs to be able to submit to COA promptly or before the 5th day of the ensuing month; and

Review the monthly RAAFs submitted by the accountable officers before submitting to COA to avoid errors therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated Municipal Treasurer to:

Reevaluate based on prior accountabilities the fidelity bond coverage to be applied for each AO and comply with the bonding requirements stated in BTr Circular No. 02-009 dated August 6, 2009; and

Implement control measures to avoid instances of collecting clerks holding funds in excess of their existing maximum bond accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers of all Accountable Officers to readily determine their accountabilities and prevent loss of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Inventory Committee to completely conduct the inventory taking or carry out the One-Time Cleansing of all properties of the Municipality as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish actual existence, ownership and condition of the PPE;
Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to execute the reconciliation of accounting and property records to accurately reflect the PPE balance in the financial statement; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to correct balances of the aforementioned accounts in the Financial Statements, and furnish COA with the corrections made therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the MSWD Officer to ensure that sufficient documentation is obtained during monitoring and liquidation of the livelihood assistance granted to various CSOs as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 and other laws, rules and regulation applicable to the transaction; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow correct accounting entry upon release and liquidation of the livelihood assistance to CSOs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to enhance transparency, accountability and avoid misuse of public funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Analyze and reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds reciprocal accounts; and

Make necessary adjustments to correct balances of the aforementioned accounts in the Financial Statements, and furnish COA with the corrections made therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Analyze and reconcile the transactions in the aforementioned accounts and follow the correct accounting entries as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and

Make necessary adjustments to correct balances of the aforementioned accounts in the Financial Statements, and furnish COA with the corrections made therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Enjoin the members of the SB to:

Comply with provisions laid under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2022-083 on the accreditation of CSOs to ensure that only qualified, registered, and duly recognized organizations shall receive livelihood assistance; and
Expedite the passage of an ordinance which streamline the procedures for granting, monitoring, and liquidating of livelihood assistance.

Instruct the MSWD Officer to ensure to obtain sufficient documentation during application and release of the livelihood assistance granted to various CSOs as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 and other laws, rules and regulation applicable to the transaction.

We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of directly contracting private individuals to act as organizers, choreographers, etc. for municipal events without adhering to the rules and procedures embodied in RA 9184 and its IRR;and
Endeavor to acquire goods and services from suppliers with legitimate business that can provide business/service permits and licenses from government institutions and PhilGEPS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to assign personnel to follow-up and monitor the distribution of checks and strictly follow the provisions of Section 107 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006; and

Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to transmit timely to the audit team the disbursement vouchers to avoid issuance of disallowance and/ or suspension of payments of salaries of the concerned personnel in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the designated HRD Officer to require the submission of a PSA-authenticated birth certificate or an equivalent document for verification of identity before hiring or renewal of the casual, job order, contractual, or consultant workers; and

Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure that wages are paid only to casual, job order, contractual, and consultant workers who possess PSA birth certificates and whose existence has been duly verified.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks; and
Practice sending written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale.
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to restore the cash to reflect the correct CIB-LCCA account balance; and
Adhere to Section 59, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs for the procedure on replacement of stale checks.

Recommendation
In view of the aforementioned observations, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor is agreeable to:
Instruct the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct a physical count of Drugs and Medicines to determine the actual inventory balance to be reflected in the Statement of Financial Position at year-end; and
Thereafter, submit to this office the RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, in compliance to Appendix 48, Volume III of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Create a sound inventory system for proper monitoring, accounting and reporting of drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies, whether it be procured or donated;
Refrain from directly charging to expense the procured drugs and medicines in compliance to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and
Henceforth, reconcile with the Inventory Committee the results of the semi-annual/annual physical count and the accounting records.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Compute the depreciation expense and the related accumulated depreciation applicable for the Land Improvement account of the Municipality in compliance to IPSAS; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts in compliance to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Management and which concurrence on the following was assured:
For high-value equipment, make a representation with the National Government Agencies (NGAs) concerned to expedite the MOA signing or secure other related documents to support the transfer of the properties. These documents will be the basis of the Accountant and the GSO in recording the properties in their respective books;
For other equipment/ materials/ supplies/ inventories found at station, instruct the appraisal committee to appraise the value of the existing donated properties. Such appraisal report shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the donated properties; and
Henceforth, furnish the GSO and the Municipal Accountant, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor, with copies of the following:
For donation from other government agencies, require the donor- agency to sign a MOA and provide the LGU with copies of the JEV reflecting the dropping of said properties from the books of account of the donor-agency;
For donations from other sources, require the donor to execute a deed of donation duly signed by the donor and the donee, reflecting thereon the type of assets being donated, the related costs and the intent for use of the said donation; and
For donations without value, instruct the appraisal committee to determine the value of the donated asset to be reflected in the Deed of Donation or MOA.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Compute the correct depreciation expense in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and
Submit Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjusting entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and concurred to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry in taking up the Investment Property as Building Account in compliance to IPSAS 16 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
Also, the management should develop criteria so that it can exercise that judgment consistently in accordance with the definition of investment property.  The criteria should also be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements when classification is difficult.

Recommendation
In view of the observations detailed above, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor and is amenable to:
Obtain copies of MOAs or its equivalent for fund transfers received in CY 2023, coordinate with the SAs concerned  and submit the same to the audit team to facilitate validation of disbursements as to propriety and timeliness in the implementation of the PPAs; and
Henceforth, refrain from disbursing from fund transfers from other NGAs without securing first the MOA to avoid suspensions of payment  in the future.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and agreement was reached to, henceforth:
Require all officers concerned to submit all contracts, regardless of amount, within the reglementary period of five (5) days from the date of its execution in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009;
Instruct the GSO to indicate the date of “conforme” in the POs to provide necessary basis in determining the obligation of Suppliers; and
Enjoin the HRMO to indicate in the contracts the date of execution or signing of all parties involved.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation	

We recommended that the Local Government Unit through the Municipal Development Council should formulate a more detailed Annual Investment Program which specifically identifies the Programs, Projects, and Activities to be funded under the 20% Development Fund. The proposed projects must include its complete description, location and period of implementation to facilitate its utilization in accordance with JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and also to enhance transparency and accountability of the LGU for the use of the 20% DF.

We also recommended that the LGU should refrain from using the 20% Development Fund in the form of prize because the said fund is intended to finance the LGU’s priority development projects which partakes the nature of investment or capital expenditure. The term prize implies giving of reward or cash expense. While it is being termed as such by the LGU to encourage performance of its Barangays, it also subverts the objective of the said funds as those non-performing Barangays could miss the opportunity to benefit from being funded of their development projects. The 20% DF shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU. Its release should not be premised on some sort of a competition but on a clearly identified development plan.

Locate the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations Obligation, and Balances prior to 2018 and check for unpaid obligations recorded therein.
In the absence of any possible claims, determine which accounts were already consummated or accomplished and revert these accounts payable to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the management should undertake the following:
Require the departments concerned to notify the GSO of replacement activities relating to the repairs and maintenance of assets and property so that the latter can initiate the preparation of required reports.
The GSO should prepare the waste materials report in three copies, original copy should be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, one copy for their office file, and the last copy to COA.
Follow the procedures contained in COA Circular NO. 89-296 for the proper disposal of property and waste materials.

We have recommended the following:
Ensure that funds for SEF are disbursed and paid under strict compliance and aligned to the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 2, s. 2020; and
Require refund of the COVID-19 allowance received by the Local School Board and reprogram the funds for project/s addressing the needs for continuity of learning and safety of students and personnel during this time of pandemic.

We recommended that Management should:
Restitute the amount given as honorarium for local school board members and discontinue the giving of such honorarium in the succeeding years.
Review the provisions of applicable laws when it comes to forming boards and committees within the LGU including the remunerations and compensations allowed. Also, it is recommended to re-organize the members of the LSB so as to include those enumerated in Section 98 of RA 7160.
Appropriate funds for the local school board operations in the General Fund and not under SEF.

We recommended that Management should:
Revisit NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 to determine the allowable expenditures for the utilization of LDRRM.
Allocate funds for MDRRMO under the General Fund for non-disaster related transactions such as but not limited to repairs and maintenance and other operating expenses.
Stop the practice of charging repairs, maintenance, and fuel to MDRRMF unless related to disaster response and rehabilitation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management should:
Strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Refrain from granting of additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should remind the Municipal Accountant to follow Section 70 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System to ensure timely submission of monthly and quarterly financial statements.
We also recommended that pending submission of the year-end financial statements, the latest trial balance of the LGU must be submitted as soon as possible for our review.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to ascertain the correctness and existence of Cash in Bank and effect the recording of any reconciling items for the fair presentation of the account in the Trial Balance and Financial Statements. The LCE might consider to augment personnel to the accounting unit to effectively address the reporting backlogs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should observe the proper accounting entries of transferred funds, especially with respect to recognition of assets as provided under Paragraphs 76-79 of PPSAS 23 and the proper use of accounts provided in the revised chart of account for LGU particularly in use of the Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets account for infrastructure projects which are still in the process of construction.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate adjusting entries to recognize the CIP account. We have prepared a proforma accounting entries for inter-agency transferred funds for your reference and guide (see Annex B).
We recommended further that a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement be submitted to this Office for review.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer should ensure that all documentary requirements were submitted by the contractor and attached to the vouchers before payment thereof is authorized.
We recommended further that the lacking documents as noted in Annex A should be submitted to our office as soon as possible.

Ensure that the fidelity bond of Accountable Officers is sufficient to secure the amount of their money accountabilities to avoid loss of government funds in case of unexpected defalcations, shortages and unrelieved losses.
Limit the amount of cash advances to the maximum liability allowed per employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the conduct of an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and the reconciliation of the list of inventories per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure (RPI).
The Local Chief Executive should direct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system.
The Municipal Engineer should provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO. This information is needed in the required reports under COA Circular 2015-008.
Separate subsidiary ledgers must be maintained for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures. This ledgers shall be kept in the Accounting division and shall be maintained for each local road.
We further recommended that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency.

The Accounting Office and GSO shall maintain a complete and updated Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively.

	Create an appraisal committee in accordance with COA Circular 92-356 and the appraised value will be used for those PPEs that do not have amount. This also will be the basis for the computation of accumulated depreciation.

	Prioritize the identification of the projects recorded in the CIP account.

	Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with copies of the report on completed projects and conduct periodic monitoring to validate the completeness of the said projects. The Municipal Accountant shall then close the CIP accounts to the appropriate asset accounts.

	Complete the inventory count by including all the PPE accounts (immovable or movable).

	Verify those assets which need a necessary entry to delete it from the records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Accounting Office should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and the authorized government depository bank to identify the benefactors who made direct deposits to the Municipality’s account.
The Municipal Accountant should prioritize the reconciliation of the bank accounts and record valid reconciling items immediately in the books.

We recommended that Management:
a.	Require the GSO to prepare and maintain the hard copies of Property Cards following the required format under Section 45 of MNGAS, Volume II and all related transactions shall be posted promptly from source documents. Also, update and complete all information in the RCPPE.
b.	Require the Accounting Office and the GSO to reconcile their records and see to it that the discrepancies noted should be immediately verified and adjusted.
c.	Verify whether donated properties were really backed up with the required documentation and prepare a JEV to record the same transaction if it has not been reflected in the Financial Statements. See to it that donations are properly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that:
a.	Management should adhere strictly with the provisions and guidelines set forth under DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and stop charging disbursements not in accordance with this Joint Circular.
b.	The Municipal Planning Officer together with Local Chief Executive and the department heads should carefully prioritize projects that need to be paid out of the 20% Development Fund.
c.	 These expenditures should be charged to the General Fund of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should make the necessary adjusting entry and the Municipal Treasurer should reimburse the 20% Development Fund account with the same amount.

We recommended to the Municipal School Board to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under R.A. No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We recommended that:
a.	The Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the limitation set by the law and the rules and procedures made by the DBM. It should also stop spending more than allowed by law. Careful planning should be done in the preparation of the budget to avoid the same mistake in the future.
b.	Management should find ways to increase its income and should intensify its collection of revenue in order to implement the increase in salary allowed by law.

We recommended to:

•Require the GSO and the Accounting Office to maintain PPELC, RPLC and    Property Cards, respectively.




•Renew the AREs of officials and employees issued more than 3 years and see to it that every transfer and issuances of equipment is properly covered by AREs.

•Reclassify and prepare adjusting entries for the small value items with more than one year serviceable life and for unserviceable items still included in the PPE account.

•Submit an application for the disposal of valueless items or auction of valuable unserviceable properties. 




•Verify whether donated properties were really backed up with the required documentation and prepare a JEV to record the same transaction if it has not been reflected in the Financial Statements. See to it that donations are properly supported with complete documentation.




•Trace in the original source document or from previous records the proper value of the items included in the Report of physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment with no assigned values.



•Determine whether those alleged properties actually found on hand and not in the list were properly recorded.
•Require the Accounting Office and the GSO to reconcile their records and see to it that the discrepancies noted should be immediately verified and adjusted.



•Hire independent appraiser to appraise the PPE and the appraised value will be used for those PPEs that do not have amount. This also will be the basis for computation of accumulated depreciation.
